quote: The beast sniffed out a goat track that wound down a defile and up along beneath a ridge, a crooked and stony way, yet wide enough for men riding single file. The Lannisters in their watchtowers got not so much a glimpse of them.
why: Best direct-text evidence for Grey Wind's scouting ability — used his wolf senses to find a hidden path that bypassed the Golden Tooth entirely.

quote: There's some say that after the battle, the king cut out Stafford Lannister's heart and fed it to the wolf.
why: Canonical rumor that cements Grey Wind's fearsome reputation and the 'wolf feeding on enemies' mythos around Robb's war identity.

quote: Even war-trained destriers went mad. Knights were trampled to death in their pavilions, and the rabble woke in terror and fled, casting aside their weapons to run the faster. Those who survived are spreading wild tales and swearing that the old gods of the north march with your brother.
why: Tyrion's eyewitness-quality account (via intelligence) of the panic Grey Wind caused — best evidence for Robb's supernatural-terror reputation at Oxcross.

quote: Sorcery is the sauce fools spoon over failure to hide the flavor of their own incompetence.
why: Signature Tyrion epigram — characterizes his rationalist worldview and his contemptuous assessment of the Lannister military failure at Oxcross.

quote: His host was raw—apprentice boys, miners, fieldhands, fisherfolk, the sweepings of Lannisport. My mutton-headed uncle had not even troubled to post sentries.
why: Best direct characterization of Stafford Lannister's military incompetence and the quality of his host — key to understanding his defeat.
